Our experiments demonstrate that grounding robotic planning in retrieved visual documents significantly outperforms baselines relying on zero-shot reasoning or few-shot example retrieval. This work establishes the basis of RAR, extending the scope of Information Retrieval from answering user queries to driving embodied physical actions.

zh.title: 通过检索-推理-行动的检索增强型机器人
zh.summary: 一个框架，使机器人能够主动检索视觉程序文档并将其接地到3D环境中以进行任务规划，在长期装配任务中明显优于零样本推理。

fr.title: Robots augmentés par récupération via Récupérer-Raisonner-Agir
fr.summary: Un framework permettant aux robots de récupérer activement la documentation procédurale visuelle et de l'ancrer dans des environnements 3D pour la planification des tâches, surpassant significativement le raisonnement sans exemple sur les tâches d'assemblage à long horizon.

de.title: Abruf-erweiterte Roboter über Abrufen-Begründen-Handeln
de.summary: Ein Framework, das Robotern ermöglicht, visuell Verfahrensdokumentation aktiv abzurufen und sie in 3D-Umgebungen für die Aufgabenplanung zu verankern, wobei es das aufgabenlose Denken bei langfristigen Montageaufgaben erheblich übertrifft.

es.title: Robots aumentados por recuperación mediante Recuperar-Razonar-Actuar
es.summary: Un framework que permite a los robots recuperar activamente documentación de procedimientos visuales y vincularla a entornos 3D para la planificación de tareas, superando significativamente el razonamiento sin ejemplos en tareas de ensamblaje de largo horizonte.

title: FinTexTS: Financial Text-Paired Time-Series Dataset via Semantic-Based and Multi-Level Pairing
summary: Large-scale financial dataset pairing stock prices with semantically-matched news at multiple contextual levels using LLMs, improving forecasting performance through sophisticated text-time-series alignment.
content:
arXiv:2603.02702v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The financial domain involves a variety of important time-series problems. Recently, time-series analysis methods that jointly leverage textual and numerical information have gained increasing attention. Accordingly, numerous efforts have been made to construct text-paired time-series datasets in the financial domain. However, financial markets are characterized by complex interdependencies, in which a company's stock price is influenced not only by company-specific events but also by events in other companies and broader macroeconomic factors. Existing approaches that pair text with financial time-series data based on simple keyword matching often fail to capture such complex relationships. To address this limitation, we propose a semantic-based and multi-level pairing framework. Specifically, we extract company-specific context for the target company from SEC filings and apply an embedding-based matching mechanism to retrieve semantically relevant news articles based on this context. Furthermore, we classify news articles into four levels (macro-level, sector-level, related company-level, and target-company level) using large language models (LLMs), enabling multi-level pairing of news articles with the target company. Applying this framework to publicly-available news datasets, we construct \textbf{FinTexTS}, a new large-scale text-paired stock price dataset. Experimental results on \textbf{FinTexTS} demonstrate the effectiveness of our semantic-based and multi-level pairing strategy in stock price forecasting. In addition to publicly-available news underlying \textbf{FinTexTS}, we show that applying our method to proprietary yet carefully curated news sources leads to higher-quality paired data and improved stock price forecasting performance.

zh.title: FinTexTS：金融文本配对时间序列数据集（通过语义基础和多层次配对）
zh.summary: 大规模金融数据集，使用大型语言模型在多个上下文级别将股票价格与语义匹配的新闻配对，通过复杂的文本-时间序列对齐改进预测性能。

fr.title: FinTexTS : Ensemble de données de séries chronologiques appariées avec texte financier via appairage sémantique et multi-niveaux
fr.summary: Grand ensemble de données financières appairant les prix des actions avec des nouvelles sémantiquement appariées à plusieurs niveaux contextuels à l'aide de modèles de langage, améliorant les performances de prévision grâce à un alignement texte-série chronologique sophistiqué.

de.title: FinTexTS: Finanztextgepaarte Zeitreihendatensatz durch semantische und mehrstufige Paarung
de.summary: Großer Finanzdatensatz, der Aktienkurse mit semantisch abgestimmten Nachrichten auf mehreren Kontextebenen unter Verwendung von LLMs paart und die Prognoseleistung durch ausgefeilte Text-Zeitreihen-Ausrichtung verbessert.

es.title: FinTexTS: Conjunto de datos de series de tiempo emparejadas con texto financiero mediante emparejamiento semántico y multinivel
es.summary: Conjunto de datos financiero a gran escala que empareja precios de acciones con noticias semánticamente coincidentes en múltiples niveles contextuales usando LLMs, mejorando el rendimiento de pronóstico a través de un alineamiento sofisticado de texto-series de tiempo.

title: A Natural Language Agentic Approach to Study Affective Polarization
summary: Multi-agent framework simulating virtual communities to study affective polarization on social media, offering flexible computational approach to analyzing complex social dynamics traditionally studied through human subjects.
content:
arXiv:2603.02711v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Affective polarization has been central to political and social studies, with growing focus on social media, where partisan divisions are often exacerbated. Real-world studies tend to have limited scope, while simulated studies suffer from insufficient high-quality training data, as manually labeling posts is labor-intensive and prone to subjective biases. The lack of adequate tools to formalize different definitions of affective polarization across studies complicates result comparison and hinders interoperable frameworks. We present a multi-agent model providing a comprehensive approach to studying affective polarization in social media. To operationalize our framework, we develop a platform leveraging large language models (LLMs) to construct virtual communities where agents engage in discussions. We showcase the potential of our platform by (1) analyzing questions related to affective polarization, as explored in social science literature, providing a fresh perspective on this phenomenon, and (2) introducing scenarios that allow observation and measurement of polarization at different levels of granularity and abstraction. Experiments show that our platform is a flexible tool for computational studies of complex social dynamics such as affective polarization. It leverages advanced agent models to simulate rich, context-sensitive interactions and systematically explore research questions traditionally addressed through human-subject studies.

zh.title: 研究情感极化的自然语言代理方法
zh.summary: 多代理框架模拟虚拟社区研究社交媒体的情感极化，提供灵活的计算方法来分析传统上通过人类受试者研究的复杂社会动力学。

fr.title: Une approche agentic du langage naturel pour étudier la polarisation affective
fr.summary: Cadre multi-agents simulant des communautés virtuelles pour étudier la polarisation affective sur les réseaux sociaux, offrant une approche computationnelle flexible pour analyser les dynamiques sociales complexes traditionnellement étudiées par des sujets humains.

de.title: Ein natürlichsprachlicher agentenbasierter Ansatz zur Untersuchung affektiver Polarisierung
de.summary: Multi-Agent-Framework, das virtuelle Gemeinschaften simuliert, um affektive Polarisierung in sozialen Medien zu untersuchen, und bietet einen flexiblen rechnerischen Ansatz zur Analyse komplexer sozialer Dynamiken, die traditionell durch menschliche Versuchspersonen untersucht werden.

es.title: Un enfoque de lenguaje natural agéntico para estudiar la polarización afectiva
es.summary: Marco multi-agente que simula comunidades virtuales para estudiar la polarización afectiva en redes sociales, ofreciendo un enfoque computacional flexible para analizar dinámicas sociales complejas tradicionalmente estudiadas a través de sujetos humanos.

title: EvoSkill: Automated Skill Discovery for Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Self-evolving framework automatically discovering reusable agent skills through failure analysis and structured refinement, achieving 7.3-12.1% accuracy improvements with demonstrated zero-shot transferability across tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.02766v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Coding agents are increasingly used as general-purpose problem solvers, but their flexibility does not by itself confer the domain expertise needed for specialized tasks. Recent work addresses this through \textit{agent skills}: reusable workflows, and code, that augment agents with domain-specific capabilities. Most skills today are hand-crafted, and existing evolutionary approaches optimize low-level artifacts (e.g. prompts \& code) that are tightly coupled to specific models and tasks. We introduce \textbf{EvoSkill}, a self-evolving framework that automatically discovers and refines agent skills through iterative failure analysis. EvoSkill analyzes execution failures, proposes new skills or edits to existing ones, and materializes them into structured, reusable skill folders. A Pareto frontier of agent programs governs selection, retaining only skills that improve held-out validation performance while the underlying model remains frozen. We evaluate EvoSkill on two benchmarks: OfficeQA, a grounded reasoning benchmark over U.S.\ Treasury data, where it improves exact-match accuracy by \textbf{7.3\%} (60.6\% $\to$ 67.9\%); and SealQA, a search-augmented QA benchmark with noisy retrieval, where it yields a \textbf{12.1\%} gain (26.6\% $\to$ 38.7\%). We also investigate the zero-shot transfer capabilties of skills evolved on one task to the other; in particular: skills evolved from SealQA transfers zero-shot to BrowseComp, improving accuracy by \textbf{5.3\%} without modification demonstrating that skill-level optimization produces transferable capabilities beyond the training task.

zh.title: EvoSkill：多代理系统中的自动化技能发现
zh.summary: 自进化框架通过故障分析和结构化改进自动发现可重用的代理技能，实现了7.3-12.1%的准确性改进，并展示了跨任务的零射击可转移性。

fr.title: EvoSkill : Découverte automatisée de compétences pour systèmes multi-agents
fr.summary: Cadre auto-évolutif découvrant automatiquement des compétences d'agents réutilisables grâce à l'analyse des défaillances et au perfectionnement structuré, réalisant des améliorations de précision de 7,3-12,1% avec une transférabilité zero-shot démontrée sur les tâches.

de.title: EvoSkill: Automatisierte Skillentdeckung für Multi-Agent-Systeme
de.summary: Selbstentwickelndes Framework, das automatisch wiederverwendbare Agent-Fähigkeiten durch Fehleranalyse und strukturierte Verfeinerung entdeckt, mit nachgewiesenen Genauigkeitsverbesserungen von 7,3-12,1% und demonstrierter Zero-Shot-Übertragbarkeit über Aufgaben hinweg.

es.title: EvoSkill: Descubrimiento automatizado de habilidades para sistemas multiagente
es.summary: Marco auto-evolucionante que descubre automáticamente habilidades de agentes reutilizables a través del análisis de fallos y refinamiento estructurado, logrando mejoras de precisión del 7,3-12,1% con transferibilidad zero-shot demostrada entre tareas.

title: Rethinking Code Similarity for Automated Algorithm Design with LLMs
summary: Novel metric measuring algorithmic similarity through problem-solving trajectories rather than code syntax, enabling better distinction of genuine algorithmic innovation and improving LLM-based algorithm design performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.02787v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rise of Large Language Model-based Automated Algorithm Design (LLM-AAD) has transformed algorithm development by autonomously generating code implementations of expert-level algorithms. Unlike traditional expert-driven algorithm development, in the LLM-AAD paradigm, the main design principle behind an algorithm is often implicitly embedded in the generated code. Therefore, assessing algorithmic similarity directly from code, distinguishing genuine algorithmic innovation from mere syntactic variation, becomes essential. While various code similarity metrics exist, they fail to capture algorithmic similarity, as they focus on surface-level syntax or output equivalence rather than the underlying algorithmic logic.
  We propose BehaveSim, a novel method to measure algorithmic similarity through the lens of problem-solving behavior as a sequence of intermediate solutions produced during execution, dubbed as problem-solving trajectories (PSTrajs). By quantifying the alignment between PSTrajs using dynamic time warping (DTW), BehaveSim distinguishes algorithms with divergent logic despite syntactic or output-level similarities. We demonstrate its utility in two key applications: (i) Enhancing LLM-AAD: Integrating BehaveSim into existing LLM-AAD frameworks (e.g., FunSearch, EoH) promotes behavioral diversity, significantly improving performance on three AAD tasks. (ii) Algorithm analysis: BehaveSim clusters generated algorithms by behavior, enabling systematic analysis of problem-solving strategies--a crucial tool for the growing ecosystem of AI-generated algorithms. Data and code of this work are open-sourced at https://github.com/RayZhhh/behavesim.

zh.title: 重新思考代码相似性以实现LLM自动化算法设计
zh.summary: 一种新颖的指标，通过问题解决轨迹而非代码语法来衡量算法相似性，能够更好地区分真正的算法创新，并提高基于LLM的算法设计性能。

fr.title: Repenser la similarité du code pour la conception automatique d'algorithmes avec les LLM
fr.summary: Une métrique nouvelle qui mesure la similarité algorithmique par les trajectoires de résolution de problèmes plutôt que par la syntaxe du code, permettant une meilleure distinction de l'innovation algorithmique véritable et améliorant les performances de conception d'algorithmes basés sur les LLM.

de.title: Umdenken der Code-Ähnlichkeit für automatisiertes Algorithmusdesign mit LLMs
de.summary: Eine neuartige Metrik, die algorithmische Ähnlichkeit durch Problemlösungsbahnen statt Code-Syntax misst, eine bessere Unterscheidung echter algorithmischer Innovation ermöglicht und die Leistung des LLM-basierten Algorithmusdesigns verbessert.

es.title: Replanteamiento de la similitud del código para el diseño automatizado de algoritmos con LLM
es.summary: Una métrica novedosa que mide la similitud algorítmica a través de trayectorias de resolución de problemas en lugar de sintaxis de código, permitiendo una mejor distinción de innovación algorítmica genuina y mejorando el rendimiento del diseño de algoritmos basado en LLM.

title: Agentified Assessment of Logical Reasoning Agents
summary: Agent-based evaluation framework enabling reproducible and auditable assessment of logical reasoning agents, achieving 86.7% accuracy on theorem proving through auto-formalization and SMT solver integration.
content:
arXiv:2603.02788v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present a framework for evaluating and benchmarking logical reasoning agents when assessment itself must be reproducible, auditable, and robust to execution failures. Building on agentified assessment, we use an assessor agent to issue tasks, enforce execution budgets, parse outputs, and record structured failure types, while the agent under test only needs to expose a standardized agent-to-agent interface. As a case study, we benchmark an auto-formalization agent for first-order logic (FOL) reasoning on a solver-verified and repaired split of FOLIO. The agent translates natural language premises and conclusions into executable Z3Py programs and employs satisfiability modulo theories (SMT) solving to determine logical entailment. On the cleaned FOLIO validation set, the auto-formalization agent achieves 86.70% accuracy under the assessor protocol, outperforming a chain-of-thought baseline (73.89%).

zh.title: Agent逻辑推理能力的认证评估
zh.summary: 基于Agent的评估框架，能够对逻辑推理Agent进行可复现和可审计的评估，通过自动形式化和SMT求解器集成在定理证明上达到86.7%的精度。

fr.title: Évaluation agentifiée de la capacité de raisonnement logique des agents
fr.summary: Cadre d'évaluation basé sur les agents permettant l'évaluation reproductible et vérifiable des agents de raisonnement logique, atteignant une précision de 86,7% sur la preuve de théorèmes grâce à l'auto-formalisation et l'intégration des solveurs SMT.

de.title: Agentifizierte Bewertung von logischen Argumentationsagenten
de.summary: Ein agentenbasiertes Bewertungsframework, das eine reproduzierbare und prüfbare Bewertung von Logik-Argumentationsagenten ermöglicht und durch Autoformalisierung und SMT-Solver-Integration 86,7% Genauigkeit beim Theorembeweis erreicht.

es.title: Evaluación agentificada del razonamiento lógico de los agentes
es.summary: Marco de evaluación basado en agentes que permite la evaluación reproducible y auditable del razonamiento lógico de los agentes, logrando una precisión del 86,7% en la demostración de teoremas a través de la formalización automática e integración del solucionador SMT.

title: Guideline-Grounded Evidence Accumulation for High-Stakes Agent Verification
summary: Agent verification framework using expert-curated clinical guidelines to evaluate high-stakes medical decisions, improving discrimination and calibration metrics on diagnostic tasks with practical clinician validation.
content:
arXiv:2603.02798v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As LLM-powered agents have been used for high-stakes decision-making, such as clinical diagnosis, it becomes critical to develop reliable verification of their decisions to facilitate trustworthy deployment. Yet, existing verifiers usually underperform owing to a lack of domain knowledge and limited calibration. To address this, we establish GLEAN, an agent verification framework with Guideline-grounded Evidence Accumulation that compiles expert-curated protocols into trajectory-informed, well-calibrated correctness signals. GLEAN evaluates the step-wise alignment with domain guidelines and aggregates multi-guideline ratings into surrogate features, which are accumulated along the trajectory and calibrated into correctness probabilities using Bayesian logistic regression. Moreover, the estimated uncertainty triggers active verification, which selectively collects additional evidence for uncertain cases via expanding guideline coverage and performing differential checks. We empirically validate GLEAN with agentic clinical diagnosis across three diseases from the MIMIC-IV dataset, surpassing the best baseline by 12% in AUROC and 50% in Brier score reduction, which confirms the effectiveness in both discrimination and calibration. In addition, the expert study with clinicians recognizes GLEAN's utility in practice.

zh.title: 基于指南的高风险Agent验证证据积累
zh.summary: Agent验证框架，使用专家策划的临床指南来评估高风险医疗决策，改进诊断任务的判别和校准指标，具有实际的临床医生验证。

fr.title: Accumulation de preuves fondée sur les lignes directrices pour la vérification des agents à enjeux élevés
fr.summary: Cadre de vérification des agents utilisant des directives cliniques sélectionnées par des experts pour évaluer les décisions médicales à enjeux élevés, améliorant les métriques de discrimination et de calibrage sur les tâches diagnostiques avec une validation pratique des cliniciens.

de.title: Richtliniengestützte Evidenzakkumulation zur Überprüfung von Hochrisikoagenten
de.summary: Agent-Verifizierungsrahmen, der von Experten kuratierte klinische Richtlinien nutzt, um hochriskante medizinische Entscheidungen zu bewerten und die Diskriminierungs- und Kalibrierungsmetriken bei Diagnoseaufgaben mit praktischer Kliniker-Validierung zu verbessern.

es.title: Acumulación de pruebas fundamentada en directrices para la verificación de agentes de alto riesgo
es.summary: Marco de verificación de agentes que utiliza directrices clínicas curadas por expertos para evaluar decisiones médicas de alto riesgo, mejorando las métricas de discriminación y calibración en tareas diagnósticas con validación clínica práctica.

title: LLM-based Argument Mining meets Argumentation and Description Logics: a Unified Framework for Reasoning about Debates
summary: Framework integrating LLM argument extraction with formal argumentation semantics and description logic querying for transparent, verifiable analysis of complex debates with explicit support and attack relations.
content:
arXiv:2603.02858v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong performance in analyzing and generating text, yet they struggle with explicit, transparent, and verifiable reasoning over complex texts such as those containing debates. In particular, they lack structured representations that capture how arguments support or attack each other and how their relative strengths determine overall acceptability. We encompass these limitations by proposing a framework that integrates learning-based argument mining with quantitative reasoning and ontology-based querying. Starting from a raw debate text, the framework extracts a fuzzy argumentative knowledge base, where arguments are explicitly represented as entities, linked by attack and support relations, and annotated with initial fuzzy strengths reflecting plausibility w.r.t. the debate's context. Quantitative argumentation semantics are then applied to compute final argument strengths by propagating the effects of supports and attacks. These results are then embedded into a fuzzy description logic setting, enabling expressive query answering through efficient rewriting techniques. The proposed approach provides a transparent, explainable, and formally grounded method for analyzing debates, overcoming purely statistical LLM-based analyses.

zh.title: 基于LLM的论证挖掘与论证和描述逻辑的结合：关于辩论推理的统一框架
zh.summary: 将LLM论证提取与形式论证语义和描述逻辑查询集成的框架，用于对复杂辩论进行透明、可验证的分析，具有明确的支持和攻击关系。

fr.title: L'extraction d'arguments basée sur LLM rencontre l'argumentation et les logiques de description : un cadre unifié pour le raisonnement sur les débats
fr.summary: Cadre intégrant l'extraction d'arguments LLM avec la sémantique de l'argumentation formelle et l'interrogation de la logique de description pour une analyse transparente et vérifiable des débats complexes avec des relations de soutien et d'attaque explicites.

de.title: LLM-basiertes Argument Mining trifft auf Argumentation und Beschreibungslogiken: Ein einheitliches Framework für die Argumentation über Debatten
de.summary: Framework, das LLM-Argumentextraktion mit formaler Argumentationssemantik und Beschreibungslogik-Abfragen integriert, um transparente, verifizierbare Analysen komplexer Debatten mit expliziten Unterstützungs- und Angriffsrelationen zu ermöglichen.

es.title: La minería de argumentos basada en LLM se encuentra con la argumentación y las lógicas de descripción: un marco unificado para razonar sobre debates
es.summary: Marco que integra la extracción de argumentos LLM con la semántica de argumentación formal y consultas de lógica de descripción para análisis transparentes y verificables de debates complejos con relaciones explícitas de apoyo y ataque.

title: Retrievit: In-context Retrieval Capabilities of Transformers, State Space Models, and Hybrid Architectures
summary: Comparative analysis showing hybrid Transformer-State Space Model architectures achieve comparable retrieval performance to Transformers with linear-time complexity, revealing fundamental differences in positional reasoning.
content:
arXiv:2603.02874v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Transformers excel at in-context retrieval but suffer from quadratic complexity with sequence length, while State Space Models (SSMs) offer efficient linear-time processing but have limited retrieval capabilities. We investigate whether hybrid architectures combining Transformers and SSMs can achieve the best of both worlds on two synthetic in-context retrieval tasks. The first task, n-gram retrieval, requires the model to identify and reproduce an n-gram that succeeds the query within the input sequence. The second task, position retrieval, presents the model with a single query token and requires it to perform a two-hop associative lookup: first locating the corresponding element in the sequence, and then outputting its positional index. Under controlled experimental conditions, we assess data efficiency, length generalization, robustness to out of domain training examples, and learned representations across Transformers, SSMs, and hybrid architectures. We find that hybrid models outperform SSMs and match or exceed Transformers in data efficiency and extrapolation for information-dense context retrieval. However, Transformers maintain superiority in position retrieval tasks. Through representation analysis, we discover that SSM-based models develop locality-aware embeddings where tokens representing adjacent positions become neighbors in embedding space, forming interpretable structures. This emergent property, absent in Transformers, explains both the strengths and limitations of SSMs and hybrids for different retrieval tasks. Our findings provide principled guidance for architecture selection based on task requirements and reveal fundamental differences in how Transformers and SSMs, and hybrid models learn positional associations.

zh.title: Retrievit：Transformer、状态空间模型和混合架构的上下文检索能力
zh.summary: 比较分析表明混合Transformer-状态空间模型架构实现了与Transformer相当的检索性能，同时具有线性时间复杂性，揭示了位置推理中的基本差异。

fr.title: Retrievit : Capacités de récupération en contexte des Transformers, des modèles d'espace d'état et des architectures hybrides
fr.summary: Une analyse comparative montrant que les architectures hybrides Transformer-State Space Model réalisent des performances de récupération comparables aux Transformers avec une complexité temporelle linéaire, révélant des différences fondamentales dans le raisonnement positionnel.

de.title: Retrievit: In-Kontext-Abruffähigkeiten von Transformers, State-Space-Modellen und hybriden Architekturen
de.summary: Vergleichende Analyse zeigt, dass Hybrid-Transformer-State-Space-Modell-Architekturen vergleichbare Abrufleistungen mit linearer Zeitkomplexität erreichen und grundlegende Unterschiede bei der Positionslogik offenbaren.

es.title: Retrievit: Capacidades de recuperación en contexto de Transformers, modelos de espacio de estados y arquitecturas híbridas
es.summary: Análisis comparativo que muestra que las arquitecturas híbridas de Transformer-State Space Model logran un rendimiento de recuperación comparable al de los Transformers con complejidad temporal lineal, revelando diferencias fundamentales en el razonamiento posicional.

title: SAE as a Crystal Ball: Interpretable Features Predict Cross-domain Transferability of LLMs without Training
summary: Metric using sparse autoencoders to forecast post-training transferability without fine-tuning, achieving high predictive accuracy and enabling informed selection of post-training strategies for cross-domain performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.02908v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In recent years, pre-trained large language models have achieved remarkable success across diverse tasks. Besides the pivotal role of self-supervised pre-training, their effectiveness in downstream applications also depends critically on the post-training process, which adapts models to task-specific data and objectives. However, this process inevitably introduces model shifts that can influence performance in different domains, and how such shifts transfer remains poorly understood. To open up the black box, we propose the SAE-based Transferability Score (STS), a new metric that leverages sparse autoencoders (SAEs) to forecast post-training transferability. Taking supervised fine-tuning as an example, STS identifies shifted dimensions in SAE representations and calculates their correlations with downstream domains, enabling reliable estimation of transferability \textit{before} fine-tuning. Extensive experiments across multiple models and domains show that STS accurately predicts the transferability of supervised fine-tuning, achieving Pearson correlation coefficients above 0.7 with actual performance changes. Beyond this, we take an initial step toward extending STS to reinforcement learning. We believe that STS can serve as an {\color{black} interpretable} tool for guiding post-training strategies in LLMs. Code is available at https://github.com/PKU-ML/STS.

zh.title: SAE作为水晶球：可解释特征预测LLM的跨域可迁移性而无需训练
zh.summary: 使用稀疏自编码器预测训练后可迁移性而无需微调的度量，实现高预测准确性，并支持跨域性能的训练后策略的知情选择。

fr.title: SAE comme une boule de cristal : les fonctionnalités interprétables prédisent la transférabilité inter-domaines des LLM sans entraînement
fr.summary: Métrique utilisant des auto-encodeurs clairsemés pour prévoir la transférabilité post-entraînement sans fine-tuning, réalisant une haute précision prédictive et permettant une sélection éclairée des stratégies post-entraînement pour la performance inter-domaines.

de.title: SAE als Kristallkugel: Interpretierbare Merkmale prognostizieren die domänenübergreifende Übertragbarkeit von LLMs ohne Training
de.summary: Metrik unter Verwendung spärlicher Autoencodierer zur Vorhersage der Übertragbarkeit nach dem Training ohne Fine-Tuning, erreichende hohe Vorhersagegenauigkeit und ermöglichende informierte Auswahl von Strategien nach dem Training für domänenübergreifende Leistung.

es.title: SAE como bola de cristal: características interpretables predicen la transferibilidad entre dominios de LLM sin entrenamiento
es.summary: Métrica que utiliza codificadores automáticos dispersos para pronosticar la transferibilidad posterior al entrenamiento sin ajuste fino, logrando alta precisión predictiva y permitiendo la selección informada de estrategias posteriores al entrenamiento para el desempeño entre dominios.

title: ShipTraj-R1: Reinforcing Ship Trajectory Prediction in Large Language Models via Group Relative Policy Optimization
summary: ShipTraj-R1 applies reinforcement learning to improve LLM-based ship trajectory prediction, reformulating the task as text-to-text generation with domain-specific prompts and rewards. The framework achieves lower prediction errors than existing deep learning and LLM baselines on real-world maritime datasets.
content:
arXiv:2603.02939v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent advancements in reinforcement fine-tuning have significantly improved the reasoning ability of large language models (LLMs). In particular, methods such as group relative policy optimization (GRPO) have demonstrated strong capabilities across various fields. However, applying LLMs to ship trajectory prediction remains largely unexplored. In this paper, we propose ShipTraj-R1, a novel LLM-based framework that reformulates ship trajectory prediction as a text-to-text generation problem. (1) We design a dynamic prompt containing trajectory information about conflicting ships to guide the model to achieve adaptive chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. (2) We introduce a comprehensive rule-based reward mechanism to incentivize the reasoning format and prediction accuracy of the model. (3) Our ShipTraj-R1 is reinforced through the GRPO mechanism guided by domain-specific prompts and rewards, and utilizes the Qwen3 as the model backbone. Extensive experimental results on two complex and real-world maritime datasets show that the proposed ShipTraj-R1 achieves the least error compared with state-of-the-art deep learning and LLM-based baselines.

zh.title: ShipTraj-R1: 通过群体相对策略优化强化大语言模型中的船舶轨迹预测
zh.summary: ShipTraj-R1应用强化学习改进基于LLM的船舶轨迹预测，将任务重新定义为具有领域特定提示和奖励的文本到文本生成。该框架在真实海事数据集上实现了比现有深度学习和LLM基准更低的预测误差。

fr.title: ShipTraj-R1: Renforcer la prédiction de trajectoire de navires dans les grands modèles de langage via l'optimisation de politique relative de groupe
fr.summary: ShipTraj-R1 applique l'apprentissage par renforcement pour améliorer la prédiction de trajectoire de navires basée sur les LLM, en reformulant la tâche comme une génération texte-à-texte avec des invites et des récompenses spécifiques au domaine. Le cadre atteint des erreurs de prédiction plus faibles que les bases de comparaison existantes du deep learning et des LLM sur les ensembles de données maritimes du monde réel.

de.title: ShipTraj-R1: Verbesserung der Schiffstrajektorienvorhersage in großen Sprachmodellen mittels Gruppenbezogener relativer Richtlinienoptimierung
de.summary: ShipTraj-R1 wendet verstärktes Lernen an, um die LLM-basierte Schiffstrajektorienvorhersage zu verbessern, indem die Aufgabe als Text-zu-Text-Generierung mit domänenspezifischen Eingabeaufforderungen und Belohnungen reformuliert wird. Das Framework erreicht niedrigere Vorhersagefehler als bestehende Deep-Learning- und LLM-Baselines bei realen maritimen Datensätzen.

es.title: ShipTraj-R1: Reforzar la predicción de trayectoria de barcos en grandes modelos de lenguaje mediante optimización de política relativa de grupo
es.summary: ShipTraj-R1 aplica aprendizaje por refuerzo para mejorar la predicción de trayectoria de barcos basada en LLM, reformulando la tarea como generación de texto a texto con indicaciones y recompensas específicas del dominio. El marco logra errores de predicción más bajos que las líneas de base existentes de aprendizaje profundo y LLM en conjuntos de datos marítimos del mundo real.

title: Architecting Trust in Artificial Epistemic Agents
summary: LLMs increasingly function as epistemic agents that curate information and shape our knowledge environment, raising concerns about their reliability and calibration to human epistemic norms. The paper proposes a framework for building trustworthy AI agents that demonstrates epistemic competence, robust falsifiability, and virtuous behaviors to support a reliable knowledge ecosystem.
content:
arXiv:2603.02960v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models increasingly function as epistemic agents -- entities that can 1) autonomously pursue epistemic goals and 2) actively shape our shared knowledge environment. They curate the information we receive, often supplanting traditional search-based methods, and are frequently used to generate both personal and deeply specialized advice. How they perform these functions, including whether they are reliable and properly calibrated to both individual and collective epistemic norms, is therefore highly consequential for the choices we make. We argue that the potential impact of epistemic AI agents on practices of knowledge creation, curation and synthesis, particularly in the context of complex multi-agent interactions, creates new informational interdependencies that necessitate a fundamental shift in evaluation and governance of AI. While a well-calibrated ecosystem could augment human judgment and collective decision-making, poorly aligned agents risk causing cognitive deskilling and epistemic drift, making the calibration of these models to human norms a high-stakes necessity. To ensure a beneficial human-AI knowledge ecosystem, we propose a framework centered on building and cultivating the trustworthiness of epistemic AI agents; aligning AI these agents with human epistemic goals; and reinforcing the surrounding socio-epistemic infrastructure. In this context, trustworthy AI agents must demonstrate epistemic competence, robust falsifiability, and epistemically virtuous behaviors, supported by technical provenance systems and "knowledge sanctuaries" designed to protect human resilience. This normative roadmap provides a path toward ensuring that future AI systems act as reliable partners in a robust and inclusive knowledge ecosystem.

zh.title: 在人工认知智能体中构建信任
zh.summary: LLM越来越多地充当认知智能体，策展信息并塑造我们的知识环境，这引发了对其可靠性和与人类认知规范校准的担忧。该论文提出了一个框架，用于构建可信的AI智能体，展示认知能力、强大的可证伪性和美德行为，以支持可靠的知识生态系统。

fr.title: Architecturer la confiance dans les agents épistémiques artificiels
fr.summary: Les LLM fonctionnent de plus en plus comme des agents épistémiques qui organisent les informations et façonnent notre environnement de connaissances, soulevant des préoccupations concernant leur fiabilité et leur calibrage aux normes épistémiques humaines. L'article propose un cadre pour construire des agents IA dignes de confiance qui démontrent une compétence épistémique, une réfutabilité robuste et des comportements vertueux pour soutenir un écosystème de connaissances fiable.

de.title: Vertrauen in künstliche epistemische Agenten aufbauen
de.summary: LLMs funktionieren zunehmend als epistemische Agenten, die Informationen kuratieren und unsere Wissensumgebung prägen, was Bedenken hinsichtlich ihrer Zuverlässigkeit und Kalibrierung zu menschlichen epistemischen Normen aufwirft. Das Papier schlägt einen Rahmen für die Erstellung vertrauenswürdiger KI-Agenten vor, die epistemische Kompetenz, robuste Falsifizierbarkeit und tugendhafte Verhaltensweisen demonstrieren, um ein zuverlässiges Wissensökosystem zu unterstützen.

es.title: Arquitectura de la confianza en agentes epistémicos artificiales
es.summary: Los LLM funcionan cada vez más como agentes epistémicos que curan información y moldean nuestro entorno de conocimiento, lo que genera preocupaciones sobre su confiabilidad y calibración con las normas epistémicas humanas. El documento propone un marco para construir agentes de IA confiables que demuestren competencia epistémica, refutabilidad robusta y comportamientos virtuosos para apoyar un ecosistema de conocimiento confiable.

title: SpatialText: A Pure-Text Cognitive Benchmark for Spatial Understanding in Large Language Models
summary: SpatialText is a diagnostic framework that tests whether LLMs genuinely construct spatial mental models or rely on linguistic heuristics. The evaluation reveals that current models struggle with egocentric perspective transformation and local reference frame reasoning, indicating they depend heavily on co-occurrence patterns rather than coherent spatial representations.
content:
arXiv:2603.03002v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Genuine spatial reasoning relies on the capacity to construct and manipulate coherent internal spatial representations, often conceptualized as mental models, rather than merely processing surface linguistic associations. While large language models exhibit advanced capabilities across various domains, existing benchmarks fail to isolate this intrinsic spatial cognition from statistical language heuristics. Furthermore, multimodal evaluations frequently conflate genuine spatial reasoning with visual perception. To systematically investigate whether models construct flexible spatial mental models, we introduce SpatialText, a theory-driven diagnostic framework. Rather than functioning simply as a dataset, SpatialText isolates text-based spatial reasoning through a dual-source methodology. It integrates human-annotated descriptions of real 3D indoor environments, which capture natural ambiguities, perspective shifts, and functional relations, with code-generated, logically precise scenes designed to probe formal spatial deduction and epistemic boundaries. Systematic evaluation across state-of-the-art models reveals fundamental representational limitations. Although models demonstrate proficiency in retrieving explicit spatial facts and operating within global, allocentric coordinate systems, they exhibit critical failures in egocentric perspective transformation and local reference frame reasoning. These systematic errors provide strong evidence that current models rely heavily on linguistic co-occurrence heuristics rather than constructing coherent, verifiable internal spatial representations. SpatialText thus serves as a rigorous instrument for diagnosing the cognitive boundaries of artificial spatial intelligence.

zh.title: SpatialText: 大语言模型空间理解的纯文本认知基准
zh.summary: SpatialText是一个诊断框架，测试LLM是否真正构建空间心理模型或依赖语言启发式方法。评估表明，当前模型在自中心视角变换和局部参考框架推理方面存在困难，表明它们严重依赖于共现模式而不是连贯的空间表征。

fr.title: SpatialText: Un benchmark cognitif pur-texte pour la compréhension spatiale dans les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: SpatialText est un cadre diagnostique qui teste si les LLM construisent véritablement des modèles mentaux spatiaux ou s'appuient sur des heuristiques linguistiques. L'évaluation révèle que les modèles actuels ont du mal avec la transformation de la perspective égocentrique et le raisonnement du cadre de référence local, ce qui indique qu'ils dépendent fortement des modèles de co-occurrence plutôt que de représentations spatiales cohérentes.

de.title: SpatialText: Ein reines Text-Kognitives Benchmark für räumliches Verständnis in großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: SpatialText ist ein diagnostisches Framework, das testet, ob LLMs echte räumliche mentale Modelle konstruieren oder sich auf linguistische Heuristiken verlassen. Die Bewertung zeigt, dass aktuelle Modelle Schwierigkeiten mit egozentrischer Perspektiventransformation und lokalem Referenzrahmendenken haben, was darauf hindeutet, dass sie stark von Ko-Auftrittsmustern abhängen, anstatt kohärente räumliche Darstellungen zu verwenden.

es.title: SpatialText: Un benchmark cognitivo de texto puro para la comprensión espacial en grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: SpatialText es un marco de diagnóstico que prueba si los LLM construyen genuinamente modelos mentales espaciales o se basan en heurísticas lingüísticas. La evaluación revela que los modelos actuales tienen dificultades con la transformación de perspectiva egocéntrica y el razonamiento del marco de referencia local, lo que indica que dependen en gran medida de patrones de co-ocurrencia en lugar de representaciones espaciales coherentes.

title: OrchMAS: Orchestrated Reasoning with Multi Collaborative Heterogeneous Scientific Expert Structured Agents
summary: OrchMAS proposes a dynamic multi-agent framework where a dedicated orchestrator analyzes tasks and constructs specialized agent pipelines with tailored prompts for scientific reasoning. The system enables iterative replanning and role reallocation based on feedback, improving robustness and specialization for knowledge-intensive domains.
content:
arXiv:2603.03005v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-agent large language model frameworks are promising for complex multi step reasoning, yet existing systems remain weak for scientific and knowledge intensive domains due to static prompts and agent roles, rigid workflows, and homogeneous model reliance, leading to poor domain adaptation, limited reasoning flexibility, and high latency on heterogeneous or long-horizon scientific tasks. They also struggle to revise earlier decisions when intermediate reasoning diverges, reducing reliability in structured and calculation heavy settings. To address these limitations, we propose a scientific domain oriented interactive two tier multi model orchestration framework. A dedicated orchestration model analyzes each task, dynamically constructs a domain aware reasoning pipeline, and instantiates specialized expert agents with tailored prompts, while an execution model performs each step under generated role and instruction specifications. The orchestrator iteratively updates the pipeline based on intermediate feedback, enabling dynamic replanning, role reallocation, and prompt refinement across multi turn interactions, strengthening robustness and specialization for scientific reasoning through structured heterogeneous model collaboration. The framework is model agnostic and supports heterogeneous LLM integration with different capacities or costs, enabling flexible performance efficiency trade offs in practical scientific deployments. Experiments show consistent improvements over existing multi agent systems and strong baselines across diverse reasoning and scientific style benchmarks.

zh.title: OrchMAS：用多协作异构科学专家结构化代理进行协调推理
zh.summary: OrchMAS提出了一个动态多代理框架，其中专门的编排器分析任务并为科学推理构建具有定制提示的专门代理管道。该系统可以基于反馈进行迭代重新规划和角色重新分配，提高知识密集型领域的稳健性和专业化程度。

fr.title: OrchMAS : Raisonnement orchestré avec des agents structurés experts scientifiques hétérogènes multi-collaboratifs
fr.summary: OrchMAS propose un cadre multi-agent dynamique où un orchestrateur dédié analyse les tâches et construit des pipelines d'agents spécialisés avec des invites personnalisées pour le raisonnement scientifique. Le système permet une replannification itérative et une réallocation des rôles basées sur les retours, améliorant la robustesse et la spécialisation pour les domaines à forte intensité de connaissances.

de.title: OrchMAS: Orchestriertes Denken mit kollaborativen heterogenen wissenschaftlichen Fachexperten-strukturierten Agenten
de.summary: OrchMAS schlägt ein dynamisches Multi-Agent-Framework vor, bei dem ein dedizierter Orchestrator Aufgaben analysiert und spezialisierte Agent-Pipelines mit maßgeschneiderten Eingabeaufforderungen für wissenschaftliches Denken konstruiert. Das System ermöglicht iterative Neuplanung und Rollenneuverteilung basierend auf Feedback und verbessert damit Robustheit und Spezialisierung für wissensintensive Bereiche.

es.title: OrchMAS: Razonamiento orquestado con agentes expertos científicos heterogéneos estructurados multi-colaborativos
es.summary: OrchMAS propone un marco multi-agente dinámico donde un orquestrador dedicado analiza tareas y construye canalizaciones de agentes especializados con instrucciones personalizadas para el razonamiento científico. El sistema permite replanificación iterativa y reasignación de roles basada en retroalimentación, mejorando la robustez y especialización para dominios de uso intensivo de conocimiento.

title: REGAL: A Registry-Driven Architecture for Deterministic Grounding of Agentic AI in Enterprise Telemetry
summary: REGAL presents an architectural pattern for grounding agentic AI systems in enterprise telemetry from version control, CI/CD, and observability platforms. The registry-driven approach combines deterministic computation with LLM operations over a bounded action space, enabling governance and semantic alignment in production environments.
content:
arXiv:2603.03018v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Enterprise engineering organizations produce high-volume, heterogeneous telemetry from version control systems, CI/CD pipelines, issue trackers, and observability platforms. Large Language Models (LLMs) enable new forms of agentic automation, but grounding such agents on private telemetry raises three practical challenges: limited model context, locally defined semantic concepts, and evolving metric interfaces.
  We present REGAL, a registry-driven architecture for deterministic grounding of agentic AI systems in enterprise telemetry. REGAL adopts an explicitly architectural approach: deterministic telemetry computation is treated as a first-class primitive, and LLMs operate over a bounded, version-controlled action space rather than raw event streams.
  The architecture combines (1) a Medallion ELT pipeline that produces replayable, semantically compressed Gold artifacts, and (2) a registry-driven compilation layer that synthesizes Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools from declarative metric definitions. The registry functions as an "interface-as-code" layer, ensuring alignment between tool specification and execution, mitigating tool drift, and embedding governance policies directly at the semantic boundary.
  A prototype implementation and case study validate the feasibility of deterministic grounding and illustrate its implications for latency, token efficiency, and operational governance. This work systematizes an architectural pattern for enterprise LLM grounding; it does not propose new learning algorithms, but rather elevates deterministic computation and semantic compilation to first-class design primitives for agentic systems.

zh.title: REGAL：一个用于企业遥测中代理AI确定性接地的注册表驱动架构
zh.summary: REGAL提出了一种架构模式，用于在来自版本控制、CI/CD和可观测性平台的企业遥测中植入代理AI系统。注册表驱动的方法将确定性计算与有限操作空间上的LLM操作结合在一起，在生产环境中实现治理和语义对齐。

fr.title: REGAL : Une architecture pilotée par le registre pour l'ancrage déterministe de l'IA agentique dans la télémétrie d'entreprise
fr.summary: REGAL présente un modèle architectural pour l'ancrage des systèmes d'IA agentique dans la télémétrie d'entreprise provenant du contrôle de version, des plates-formes CI/CD et d'observabilité. L'approche pilotée par le registre combine le calcul déterministe avec les opérations LLM sur un espace d'action limité, permettant la gouvernance et l'alignement sémantique dans les environnements de production.

de.title: REGAL: Eine registrierungsgesteuerte Architektur für deterministische Verankerung von agentenbasierter KI in der Enterprise-Telemetrie
de.summary: REGAL präsentiert ein Architekturmuster zur Verankerung von agentenbasierten KI-Systemen in Enterprise-Telemetrie von Versionskontrolle, CI/CD- und Observability-Plattformen. Der registergesteuerte Ansatz kombiniert deterministische Berechnung mit LLM-Operationen über einen begrenzten Aktionsraum und ermöglicht Governance und semantische Ausrichtung in Produktionsumgebungen.

es.title: REGAL: Una arquitectura impulsada por registro para el anclaje determinista de IA agentiva en telemetría empresarial
es.summary: REGAL presenta un patrón arquitectónico para anclar sistemas de IA agentiva en telemetría empresarial de control de versiones, plataformas CI/CD y observabilidad. El enfoque impulsado por registro combina cálculo determinista con operaciones de LLM sobre un espacio de acción acotado, habilitando gobernanza y alineación semántica en entornos de producción.

title: TikZilla: Scaling Text-to-TikZ with High-Quality Data and Reinforcement Learning
summary: TikZilla, a family of Qwen models, uses supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning with image-based reward signals to generate scientific figures from text descriptions. The approach outperforms GPT-4o on human evaluation while maintaining smaller model sizes through improved training data and semantic feedback.
content:
arXiv:2603.03072v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to assist scientists across diverse workflows. A key challenge is generating high-quality figures from textual descriptions, often represented as TikZ programs that can be rendered as scientific images. Prior research has proposed a variety of datasets and modeling approaches for this task. However, existing datasets for Text-to-TikZ are too small and noisy to capture the complexity of TikZ, causing mismatches between text and rendered figures. Moreover, prior approaches rely solely on supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which does not expose the model to the rendered semantics of the figure, often resulting in errors such as looping, irrelevant content, and incorrect spatial relations. To address these issues, we construct DaTikZ-V4, a dataset more than four times larger and substantially higher in quality than DaTikZ-V3, enriched with LLM-generated figure descriptions. Using this dataset, we train TikZilla, a family of small open-source Qwen models (3B and 8B) with a two-stage pipeline of SFT followed by reinforcement learning (RL). For RL, we leverage an image encoder trained via inverse graphics to provide semantically faithful reward signals. Extensive human evaluations with over 1,000 judgments show that TikZilla improves by 1.5-2 points over its base models on a 5-point scale, surpasses GPT-4o by 0.5 points, and matches GPT-5 in the image-based evaluation, while operating at much smaller model sizes. Code, data, and models will be made available.

zh.title: TikZilla：用高质量数据和强化学习扩展文本到TikZ的转换
zh.summary: TikZilla是一个Qwen模型家族，采用监督微调，然后是带有基于图像奖励信号的强化学习，从文本描述生成科学图表。该方法在人类评估中优于GPT-4o，同时通过改进的训练数据和语义反馈保持较小的模型尺寸。

fr.title: TikZilla : Mise à l'échelle de Text-to-TikZ avec des données de haute qualité et apprentissage par renforcement
fr.summary: TikZilla, une famille de modèles Qwen, utilise l'affinage supervisé suivi d'un apprentissage par renforcement avec des signaux de récompense basés sur les images pour générer des figures scientifiques à partir de descriptions textuelles. L'approche surpasse GPT-4o lors d'une évaluation humaine tout en maintenant des tailles de modèle plus petites grâce à des données d'entraînement améliorées et des retours sémantiques.

de.title: TikZilla: Skalierung von Text-zu-TikZ mit hochwertigen Daten und Verstärkungslernen
de.summary: TikZilla, eine Familie von Qwen-Modellen, nutzt überwachtes Fine-Tuning gefolgt von Verstärkungslernen mit bildgestützten Belohnungssignalen, um wissenschaftliche Abbildungen aus Textbeschreibungen zu generieren. Der Ansatz übertrifft GPT-4o bei der Bewertung durch Menschen und behält gleichzeitig kleinere Modellgrößen durch verbesserte Trainingsdaten und semantisches Feedback bei.

es.title: TikZilla: Escalado de Text-to-TikZ con datos de alta calidad y aprendizaje por refuerzo
es.summary: TikZilla, una familia de modelos Qwen, utiliza ajuste fino supervisado seguido de aprendizaje por refuerzo con señales de recompensa basadas en imágenes para generar figuras científicas a partir de descripciones de texto. El enfoque supera a GPT-4o en evaluación humana mientras mantiene tamaños de modelo más pequeños a través de datos de entrenamiento mejorados y retroalimentación semántica.

title: RAPO: Expanding Exploration for LLM Agents via Retrieval-Augmented Policy Optimization
summary: RAPO introduces a reinforcement learning framework that enhances exploration in agentic AI by retrieving off-policy trajectories during training. The method achieves 5% average improvements across reasoning tasks while delivering 1.2x faster training by balancing self-generated outputs with retrieved step-level behaviors.
content:
arXiv:2603.03078v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic Reinforcement Learning (Agentic RL) has shown remarkable potential in large language model-based (LLM) agents. These works can empower LLM agents to tackle complex tasks via multi-step, tool-integrated reasoning. However, an inherent limitation of existing Agentic RL methods is their reliance on a pure on-policy paradigm for exploration, restricting exploration to the agent's self-generated outputs and preventing the discovery of new reasoning perspectives for further improvement. While recent efforts incorporate auxiliary off-policy signals to enhance exploration, they typically utilize full off-policy trajectories for trajectory-level policy estimation, overlooking the necessity for the fine-grained, step-level exploratory dynamics within agentic rollout. In this paper, we revisit exploration in Agentic RL and propose Retrieval-Augmented Policy Optimization (RAPO), a novel RL framework that introduces retrieval to explicitly expand exploration during training. To achieve this, we decompose the Agentic RL training process into two phases: (i) Hybrid-policy Agentic Rollout, and (ii) Retrieval-aware Policy Optimization. Specifically, we propose a Hybrid-policy Agentic Rollout strategy, which allows the agents to continuously reason over the retrieved off-policy step-level traces. It dynamically extends the reasoning receptive field of agents, enabling broader exploration conditioned on external behaviors. Subsequently, we introduce the Retrieval-aware Policy Optimization mechanism, which calibrates the policy gradient estimation with retrieval reward and importance shaping, stabilizing training and prioritizing retrieval-illuminating exploration. Extensive experiments show that RAPO achieves an +5.0% average gain on fourteen datasets across three agentic reasoning tasks, while delivering 1.2x faster training efficiency.

zh.title: RAPO：通过检索增强策略优化扩展LLM智能体的探索能力
zh.summary: RAPO引入了一个强化学习框架，通过在训练期间检索非策略轨迹来增强智能AI中的探索。该方法在推理任务中实现了5%的平均改进，同时通过平衡自生成输出与检索的步级行为实现了1.2倍更快的训练速度。

fr.title: RAPO : Expansion de l'exploration des agents LLM via l'optimisation des politiques augmentée par récupération
fr.summary: RAPO introduit un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement qui améliore l'exploration dans l'IA agentive en récupérant les trajectoires hors politique lors de l'entraînement. La méthode réalise des améliorations moyennes de 5% sur les tâches de raisonnement tout en offrant un entraînement 1,2 fois plus rapide en équilibrant les sorties auto-générées avec les comportements au niveau des étapes récupérées.

de.title: RAPO: Erweiterung der Erkundung für LLM-Agenten durch Abruf-verstärkte Politikoptimierung
de.summary: RAPO stellt ein Verstärkungslernframework vor, das die Erkundung in agentengestützter KI durch Abrufen von Off-Policy-Trajektorien während des Trainings verbessert. Das Verfahren erreicht durchschnittliche Verbesserungen von 5% bei Reasoning-Aufgaben und bietet gleichzeitig 1,2-mal schnelleres Training, indem es selbst generierte Ausgaben mit abgerufenen schrittsweisen Verhaltensweisen ausbalanciert.

es.title: RAPO: Expansión de la exploración para agentes LLM mediante optimización de políticas aumentada por recuperación
es.summary: RAPO introduce un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo que mejora la exploración en IA agentiva recuperando trayectorias fuera de política durante el entrenamiento. El método logra mejoras promedio del 5% en tareas de razonamiento mientras proporciona entrenamiento 1.2 veces más rápido al equilibrar salidas autogeneradas con comportamientos a nivel de pasos recuperados.

title: Beyond Factual Correctness: Mitigating Preference-Inconsistent Explanations in Explainable Recommendation
summary: PURE addresses a gap in LLM-based recommenders where explanations are factually correct but justify items using attributes that conflict with user preferences. The framework selects multi-hop reasoning paths aligned with user preference structure, reducing preference-inconsistent explanations while maintaining recommendation quality.
content:
arXiv:2603.03080v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM-based explainable recommenders can produce fluent explanations that are factually correct, yet still justify items using attributes that conflict with a user's historical preferences. Such preference-inconsistent explanations yield logically valid but unconvincing reasoning and are largely missed by standard hallucination or faithfulness metrics. We formalize this failure mode and propose PURE, a preference-aware reasoning framework following a select-then-generate paradigm. Instead of only improving generation, PURE intervenes in evidence selection, it selects a compact set of multi-hop item-centric reasoning paths that are both factually grounded and aligned with user preference structure, guided by user intent, specificity, and diversity to suppress generic, weakly personalized evidence. The selected evidence is then injected into LLM generation via structure-aware prompting that preserves relational constraints. To measure preference inconsistency, we introduce a feature-level, user-centric evaluation metric that reveals misalignment overlooked by factuality-based measures. Experiments on three real-world datasets show that PURE consistently reduces preference-inconsistent explanations and factual hallucinations while maintaining competitive recommendation accuracy, explanation quality, and inference efficiency. These results highlight that trustworthy explanations require not only factual correctness but also justification aligned with user preferences.

zh.title: 超越事实正确性：在可解释推荐中缓解偏好不一致的解释
zh.summary: PURE解决了LLM推荐系统中的一个差距，其中解释虽然事实正确，但使用与用户偏好冲突的属性来证明推荐项目。该框架选择与用户偏好结构相一致的多跳推理路径，减少偏好不一致的解释，同时保持推荐质量。

fr.title: Au-delà de la correction factuelle : Atténuer les explications incohérentes avec les préférences dans la recommandation explicable
fr.summary: PURE résout une lacune dans les systèmes de recommandation basés sur les LLM où les explications sont factuellement correctes mais justifient les articles en utilisant des attributs qui entrent en conflit avec les préférences de l'utilisateur. Le cadre sélectionne les chemins de raisonnement multi-sauts alignés avec la structure des préférences de l'utilisateur, réduisant les explications incohérentes avec les préférences tout en maintenant la qualité des recommandations.

de.title: Jenseits der faktischen Korrektheit: Minderung von präferenzinkonsistenten Erklärungen in erklärbarer Empfehlung
de.summary: PURE behebt einen Mangel bei LLM-basierten Empfehlungssystemen, bei denen Erklärungen faktisch korrekt sind, aber Elemente mithilfe von Attributen rechtfertigen, die den Benutzervorlieben widersprechen. Das Framework wählt mehrstufige Reasonings-Pfade aus, die mit der Benutzerpreferenzstruktur übereinstimmen, und reduziert präferenzinkonsistente Erklärungen, während die Empfehlungsqualität erhalten bleibt.

es.title: Más allá de la corrección factual: Mitigación de explicaciones inconsistentes con las preferencias en recomendaciones explicables
es.summary: PURE aborda una brecha en los sistemas de recomendación basados en LLM donde las explicaciones son factualmente correctas pero justifican elementos usando atributos que entran en conflicto con las preferencias del usuario. El marco selecciona caminos de razonamiento multi-salto alineados con la estructura de preferencias del usuario, reduciendo explicaciones inconsistentes con las preferencias mientras se mantiene la calidad de la recomendación.

title: Odin: Multi-Signal Graph Intelligence for Autonomous Discovery in Knowledge Graphs
summary: Odin is a production-deployed graph intelligence engine that enables autonomous pattern discovery in knowledge graphs without predefined queries. It combines structural importance, semantic plausibility, temporal relevance, and community-aware guidance to navigate complex knowledge structures while maintaining hallucination-free traceability for regulated industries.
content:
arXiv:2603.03097v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present Odin, the first production-deployed graph intelligence engine for autonomous discovery of meaningful patterns in knowledge graphs without prior specification. Unlike retrieval-based systems that answer predefined queries, Odin guides exploration through the COMPASS (Composite Oriented Multi-signal Path Assessment) score, a novel metric that combines (1) structural importance via Personalized PageRank, (2) semantic plausibility through Neural Probabilistic Logic Learning (NPLL) used as a discriminative filter rather than generative model, (3) temporal relevance with configurable decay, and (4) community-aware guidance through GNN-identified bridge entities and inter-community affinity scores. This multi-signal integration, particularly the bridge scoring mechanism, addresses the "echo chamber" problem where graph exploration becomes trapped in dense local communities. We formalize the autonomous discovery problem, prove theoretical properties of our scoring function, and demonstrate that beam search with multi-signal guidance achieves $O(b \cdot h)$ complexity while maintaining high recall compared to exhaustive exploration. To our knowledge, Odin represents the first autonomous discovery system deployed in regulated production environments (healthcare and insurance), demonstrating significant improvements in pattern discovery quality and analyst efficiency. Our approach maintains complete provenance traceability -- a critical requirement for regulated industries where hallucination is unacceptable.

zh.title: Odin：用于知识图谱自主发现的多信号图智能
zh.summary: Odin是一个生产部署的图智能引擎，能够在知识图谱中实现自主模式发现，无需预定义查询。它结合了结构重要性、语义合理性、时间相关性和社区感知指导，在导航复杂知识结构的同时，为受管制行业维持无幻觉的可追溯性。

fr.title: Odin : Intelligence de graphe multi-signaux pour la découverte autonome dans les graphes de connaissances
fr.summary: Odin est un moteur d'intelligence de graphe déployé en production qui permet la découverte autonome de motifs dans les graphes de connaissances sans requêtes prédéfinies. Il combine l'importance structurelle, la plausibilité sémantique, la pertinence temporelle et les conseils tenant compte de la communauté pour naviguer dans les structures de connaissances complexes tout en maintenant la traçabilité sans hallucinations pour les industries réglementées.

de.title: Odin: Multi-Signal-Graph-Intelligence für autonome Entdeckung in Wissensgraphen
de.summary: Odin ist ein produktionsbereitetes Graph-Intelligence-System, das autonome Mustererkennung in Wissensgraphen ohne vordefinierte Abfragen ermöglicht. Es kombiniert strukturelle Bedeutung, semantische Plausibilität, zeitliche Relevanz und gemeinschaftsbewusste Anleitung, um komplexe Wissensstrukturen zu navigieren und gleichzeitig halluzinationsfreie Rückverfolgbarkeit für regulierte Industrien zu gewährleisten.

es.title: Odin: Inteligencia de Gráfos Multi-Señal para Descubrimiento Autónomo en Gráfos de Conocimiento
es.summary: Odin es un motor de inteligencia de gráfos implementado en producción que permite el descubrimiento autónomo de patrones en gráfos de conocimiento sin consultas predefinidas. Combina importancia estructural, plausibilidad semántica, relevancia temporal y orientación consciente de la comunidad para navegar estructuras de conocimiento complejas mientras mantiene trazabilidad libre de alucinaciones para industrias reguladas.

title: Beyond Task Completion: Revealing Corrupt Success in LLM Agents through Procedure-Aware Evaluation
summary: Procedure-Aware Evaluation reveals that 27-78% of reported successes in LLM agent benchmarks are "corrupt successes" that violate procedural integrity despite completing tasks. The framework evaluates agents across utility, efficiency, interaction quality, and procedural integrity, exposing distinctive failure patterns across different models.
content:
arXiv:2603.03116v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents are increasingly adopted in high-stakes settings, but current benchmarks evaluate mainly whether a task was completed, not how. We introduce Procedure-Aware Evaluation (PAE), a framework that formalizes agent procedures as structured observations and exposes consistency relationships between what agents observe, communicate, and execute. PAE evaluates agents along complementary axes (Utility, Efficiency, Interaction Quality, Procedural Integrity) and applies multi-dimensional gating that categorically disqualifies corrupt outcomes. Evaluating state-of-the-art LLM agents on tau-bench yields findings at the axis, compliance, and benchmark levels. At the axis level, the dimensions capture non-redundant failure modes: utility masks reliability gaps, speed does not imply precision, and conciseness does not predict intent adherence. At the procedural compliance level, 27-78% of benchmark reported successes are corrupt successes concealing violations across interaction and integrity. Furthermore, gating substantially collapses Pass^4 rate and affects model rankings. The analysis of corrupt success cases reveals distinctive per-model failure signatures: GPT-5 spreads errors across policy, execution, and intent dimensions; Kimi-K2-Thinking concentrates 78% of violations in policy faithfulness and compliance; and Mistral-Large-3 is dominated by faithfulness failures. At the benchmark level, our analysis exposes structural flaws in the benchmark design, including task scope gaps, contradictory reward signals, and simulator artifacts that produce accidental successes.

zh.title: 超越任务完成：通过程序感知评估揭示LLM代理中的腐败成功
zh.summary: 程序感知评估揭示了LLM代理基准中报告的成功中有27-78%是"腐败成功"，尽管完成了任务但违反了程序完整性。该框架在实用性、效率、交互质量和程序完整性方面评估代理，揭露了不同模型中的独特失败模式。

fr.title: Au-delà de l'achèvement des tâches : Révéler les succès corrompus dans les agents LLM grâce à l'évaluation consciente des procédures
fr.summary: L'évaluation consciente des procédures révèle que 27-78% des succès rapportés dans les benchmarks des agents LLM sont des "succès corrompus" qui violent l'intégrité procédurale malgré l'achèvement des tâches. Le cadre évalue les agents sur l'utilité, l'efficacité, la qualité de l'interaction et l'intégrité procédurale, exposant des modèles d'échec distincts entre les différents modèles.

de.title: Über die Aufgabenvollendung hinaus: Offenlegung korrupter Erfolge in LLM-Agenten durch verfahrensbewusste Bewertung
de.summary: Die verfahrensbewusste Bewertung zeigt, dass 27-78% der gemeldeten Erfolge in LLM-Agent-Benchmarks "korrupte Erfolge" sind, die die verfahrensmäßige Integrität trotz Aufgabenvollendung verletzen. Das Framework bewertet Agenten hinsichtlich Nutzen, Effizienz, Interaktionsqualität und verfahrensmäßiger Integrität und legt charakteristische Fehlermuster verschiedener Modelle offen.

es.title: Más allá de la finalización de tareas: Revelando el éxito corrupto en agentes LLM a través de evaluación consciente de procedimientos
es.summary: La evaluación consciente de procedimientos revela que el 27-78% de los éxitos reportados en los puntos de referencia de agentes LLM son "éxitos corruptos" que violan la integridad procesal a pesar de completar las tareas. El marco evalúa agentes en utilidad, eficiencia, calidad de interacción e integridad procedural, exponiendo patrones de fallo distintivos entre diferentes modelos.

title: AI Space Physics: Constitutive boundary semantics for open AI institutions
summary: AI Space Physics formalizes governance semantics for persistent agentic AI institutions that accumulate state and expand their authority surface over time. The framework treats expansion transitions as governance-relevant events even without immediate external changes, requiring witness completeness and replayable reconstruction of adjudication.
content:
arXiv:2603.03119v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic AI deployments increasingly behave as persistent institutions rather than one-shot inference endpoints: they accumulate state, invoke external tools, coordinate multiple runtimes, and modify their future authority surface over time. Existing governance language typically specifies decision-layer constraints but leaves the causal mechanics of boundary crossing underdefined, particularly for transitions that do not immediately change the external world yet expand what the institution can later do.
  This paper introduces AI Space Physics as a constitutive semantics for open, self-expanding AI institutions. We define a minimal state model with typed boundary channels, horizon-limited reach semantics, and a membrane-witness discipline. The core law family (P-1, P-1a, P-1b, P-1c) requires witness completeness, non-bypass mediation, atomic adjudication-to-effect transitions, and replayable reconstruction of adjudication class. We explicitly separate second-order effects into structural expansion and policy broadening, and treat expansion transitions as governance-relevant even when immediate external deltas are zero.
  The novelty claim is precise rather than expansive: this work does not introduce mediation as a concept; it reclassifies authority-surface expansion as a first-class boundary event with constitutive witness obligations. In this semantics, expansion without immediate commit remains adjudication-relevant.

zh.title: AI空间物理学：开放AI机构的构成性边界语义
zh.summary: AI空间物理学形式化了持久代理AI机构的治理语义，这些机构积累状态并随时间扩展其权力范围。该框架将扩展转换视为治理相关事件，即使没有立即的外部变化，也需要见证完整性和可重放的仲裁重建。

fr.title: Physique Spatiale de l'IA : Sémantique des frontières constitutives pour les institutions IA ouvertes
fr.summary: Physique Spatiale de l'IA formalise la sémantique de gouvernance pour les institutions IA agentives persistantes qui accumulent l'état et étendent leur surface d'autorité au fil du temps. Le cadre traite les transitions d'expansion comme des événements pertinents pour la gouvernance même sans changements externes immédiats, nécessitant l'exhaustivité des témoins et la reconstruction rejouable de l'arbitrage.

de.title: KI-Raumphysik: Konstitutive Grenzensemantik für offene KI-Institutionen
de.summary: KI-Raumphysik formalisiert Governance-Semantik für persistente agentive KI-Institutionen, die Zustand ansammeln und ihre Autoritätsfläche im Laufe der Zeit erweitern. Das Framework behandelt Expansionsübergänge als governance-relevante Ereignisse auch ohne unmittelbare externe Änderungen und erfordert Zeugnissollständigkeit und wiederholbare Rekonstruktion der Schiedsspruchsfällung.

es.title: Física Espacial de IA: Semántica de límites constitutivos para instituciones IA abiertas
es.summary: Física Espacial de IA formaliza la semántica de gobernanza para instituciones IA agentivas persistentes que acumulan estado y expanden su superficie de autoridad a lo largo del tiempo. El marco trata las transiciones de expansión como eventos relevantes para la gobernanza incluso sin cambios externos inmediatos, requiriendo completitud de testigos y reconstrucción repetible del arbitraje.

title: Agentic AI-based Coverage Closure for Formal Verification
summary: This study presents an agentic AI workflow using LLMs to automate coverage analysis in formal chip verification, identifying gaps and generating required formal properties. The approach accelerates verification efficiency with measurable improvements in coverage metrics, particularly for complex designs.
content:
arXiv:2603.03147v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Coverage closure is a critical requirement in Integrated Chip (IC) development process and key metric for verification sign-off. However, traditional exhaustive approaches often fail to achieve full coverage within project timelines. This study presents an agentic AI-driven workflow that utilizes Large Language Model (LLM)-enabled Generative AI (GenAI) to automate coverage analysis for formal verification, identify coverage gaps, and generate the required formal properties. The framework accelerates verification efficiency by systematically addressing coverage holes. Benchmarking open-source and internal designs reveals a measurable increase in coverage metrics, with improvements correlated to the complexity of the design. Comparative analysis validates the effectiveness of this approach. These results highlight the potential of agentic AI-based techniques to improve formal verification productivity and support comprehensive coverage closure.

zh.title: 基于代理AI的形式验证覆盖闭包
zh.summary: 本研究展示了一个使用LLM的代理AI工作流，用于自动化形式芯片验证中的覆盖分析，识别漏洞并生成所需的形式性质。该方法加速了验证效率，在覆盖指标方面有可测量的改进，特别是对于复杂设计。

fr.title: Fermeture de couverture basée sur l'IA agentive pour la vérification formelle
fr.summary: Cette étude présente un flux de travail IA agentive utilisant les LLM pour automatiser l'analyse de couverture dans la vérification formelle des puces, en identifiant les lacunes et en générant les propriétés formelles requises. L'approche accélère l'efficacité de la vérification avec des améliorations mesurables dans les métriques de couverture, en particulier pour les conceptions complexes.

de.title: Agentive KI-basierter Abdeckungsschluss für formale Verifikation
de.summary: Diese Studie präsentiert einen agentiven KI-Workflow unter Verwendung von LLMs zur Automatisierung der Abdeckungsanalyse in der formalen Chipverifikation, Identifizierung von Lücken und Generierung erforderlicher formaler Eigenschaften. Der Ansatz beschleunigt die Verifikationseffizienz mit messbaren Verbesserungen der Abdeckungsmetriken, besonders für komplexe Designs.

es.title: Cierre de cobertura basado en IA agentiva para verificación formal
es.summary: Este estudio presenta un flujo de trabajo de IA agentiva usando LLM para automatizar el análisis de cobertura en la verificación formal de chips, identificando brechas y generando propiedades formales requeridas. El enfoque acelera la eficiencia de verificación con mejoras medibles en métricas de cobertura, particularmente para diseños complejos.

title: Saarthi for AGI: Towards Domain-Specific General Intelligence for Formal Verification
summary: Researchers present enhancements to Saarthi, an agentic AI framework for formal verification, including structured rulesets for SystemVerilog assertion generation and advanced RAG techniques. Benchmarking shows 70% improvement in assertion accuracy and 50% reduction in iterations needed for coverage closure. The work demonstrates progress toward domain-specific AI intelligence for verification tasks, addressing current LLM limitations in complex technical domains.
content:
arXiv:2603.03175v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Saarthi is an agentic AI framework that uses multi-agent collaboration to perform end-to-end formal verification. Even though the framework provides a complete flow from specification to coverage closure, with around 40% efficacy, there are several challenges that need to be addressed to make it more robust and reliable. Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) is still a distant goal, and current Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents are prone to hallucinations and making mistakes, especially when dealing with complex tasks such as formal verification. However, with the right enhancements and improvements, we believe that Saarthi can be a significant step towards achieving domain-specific general intelligence for formal verification. Especially for problems that require Short Term, Short Context (STSC) capabilities, such as formal verification, Saarthi can be a powerful tool to assist verification engineers in their work. In this paper, we present two key enhancements to the Saarthi framework: (1) a structured rulebook and specification grammar to improve the accuracy and controllability of SystemVerilog Assertion (SVA) generation, and (2) integration of advanced Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) techniques, such as GraphRAG, to provide agents with access to technical knowledge and best practices for iterative refinement and improvement of outputs. We also benchmark these enhancements for the overall Saarthi framework using challenging test cases from NVIDIA's CVDP benchmark targeting formal verification. Our benchmark results stand out with a 70% improvement in the accuracy of generated assertions, and a 50% reduction in the number of iterations required to achieve coverage closure.

zh.title: Saarthi for AGI: 走向形式化验证的领域特定通用智能
zh.summary: 研究人员展示了对Saarthi的增强，这是一个用于形式化验证的代理AI框架，包括用于SystemVerilog断言生成的结构化规则集和高级RAG技术。基准测试显示断言准确性提高70%，覆盖闭包所需迭代次数减少50%。该工作展示了在验证任务中朝向领域特定AI智能发展的进展，解决了当前大型语言模型在复杂技术领域的局限性。

fr.title: Saarthi pour AGI: Vers une intelligence générale spécifique au domaine pour la vérification formelle
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent des améliorations apportées à Saarthi, un cadre d'IA agentive pour la vérification formelle, incluant des ensembles de règles structurées pour la génération d'assertions SystemVerilog et des techniques RAG avancées. Les tests de référence montrent une amélioration de 70% de la précision des assertions et une réduction de 50% du nombre d'itérations nécessaires pour la fermeture de couverture. Le travail démontre les progrès vers une intelligence IA spécifique au domaine pour les tâches de vérification, abordant les limitations actuelles des LLM dans les domaines techniques complexes.

de.title: Saarthi für AGI: Hin zu domänenspezifischer allgemeiner Intelligenz für formale Verifikation
de.summary: Forscher präsentieren Verbesserungen an Saarthi, einem agentiven KI-Framework für formale Verifikation, einschließlich strukturierter Regelsätze für die Generierung von SystemVerilog-Assertions und fortgeschrittener RAG-Techniken. Benchmarks zeigen eine 70%ige Verbesserung der Assertion-Genauigkeit und eine 50%ige Reduzierung der für die Abdeckungsabschließung erforderlichen Iterationen. Die Arbeit demonstriert Fortschritte hin zu domänenspezifischer KI-Intelligenz für Verifikationsaufgaben und adressiert aktuelle LLM-Limitierungen in komplexen technischen Domänen.

es.title: Saarthi para AGI: Hacia una inteligencia general específica del dominio para la verificación formal
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan mejoras al marco Saarthi, un framework de IA agencial para verificación formal, incluyendo conjuntos de reglas estructuradas para la generación de aserciones SystemVerilog y técnicas RAG avanzadas. Las pruebas de referencia muestran una mejora del 70% en la precisión de las aserciones y una reducción del 50% en las iteraciones necesarias para el cierre de cobertura. Este trabajo demuestra el progreso hacia una inteligencia de IA específica del dominio para tareas de verificación, abordando las limitaciones actuales de LLM en dominios técnicos complejos.

title: FEAST: Retrieval-Augmented Multi-Hierarchical Food Classification for the FoodEx2 System
